moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to diversify single name holdings.
Increase QQQI to 20% for enhanced income and stability.
Add XLK at 8% for exposure to a high-quality tech index.
Maintain a cash buffer for capital preservation and flexibility.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect modest returns with reduced volatility.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and reduce SPAL to 3% to manage leveraged ETF risk.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to diversify single name exposure.
Add LRCX at 7% and XLK at 10% from strong scanner setups.
Balance broad index, quality tech, and limited leverage for growth.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% monthly growth with a risk-first approach. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. Expect moderate returns with controlled risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining exposure.
Increase SOXL to 8% to leverage strong semiconductor setups.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 15%, to press a winning position.
Buy LRCX at 10%, ASML at 7%, and XLK at 10% from top scanner setups.
Concentrate in high-scoring setups and use leverage for growth.
Maintain a small cash buffer with strict stop discipline.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly growth, leaning into leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher drawdown risk, especially with leveraged ETFs that decay daily. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage potential rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and enhance stability.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD and reduce SMH to 5% to cut high-beta single names and sectors.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% for enhanced monthly distributions.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
Keep a substantial cash buffer for capital preservation.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% monthly, focusing on steady distributions and capital preservation. It minimizes leverage and high-beta assets for lower drawdown. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market downturns, so it's not a savings account.
